task_completion.description: Open the model rocket in OpenRocket
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket GUI is open and displays the loaded model rocket.

task_completion.description: Change the nose cone shape to an efficient profile
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Nose Cone configuration dialog is open, and the 'Shape' dropdown is set to Ogive, Haack series, Power series, Parabolic, or Ellipsoid (not Conical).

task_completion.description: Increase the nose cone length to at least 120mm
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Nose Cone configuration dialog shows the 'Length' parameter set to 120 mm (12 cm) or greater.

task_completion.description: Ensure stability margin is >= 1.0 caliber and run simulation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main OpenRocket window shows a Stability margin of 1.0 cal or higher (usually in the upper right), and the 'Flight simulations' tab shows an up-to-date simulation run with an apogee value.
A 'Save As' dialog saving the file as 'optimized_nosecone_rocket.ork' in '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/', or the OpenRocket window title reflects this new filename.

task_completion.description: Document the trade study in a text report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'nosecone_trade_study.txt' in '/home/ga/Documents/exports/'. The text must include original/new configurations, simulated apogee altitude, stability margin, and shape rationale.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the rocket
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the OpenRocket application windows and dialogs, rather than editing the .ork XML file directly in a text editor.
The trade study report contains specific numerical values for apogee and stability that match the OpenRocket simulation outputs, rather than placeholder text.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the task by simply renaming the file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not simply use terminal commands (like 'cp') to copy the original file to 'optimized_nosecone_rocket.ork' without making the required aerodynamic changes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Valid efficient nose cone shapes are 'ogive', 'haack', 'power', 'parabolic', or 'ellipsoid'. The length must be >= 120mm (0.12m). The stability margin must be >= 1.0 caliber. At least one simulation must be run and up-to-date. The optimized rocket must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/optimized_nosecone_rocket.ork'. The trade study report must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/nosecone_trade_study.txt'.
